title: UDLM DCMGroup (instance)
description: The universal grouping record (observability/universal-groups.md): ONE entity for all grouping — Tenants, Resource Groups, policy collections, federations, cross-tenant authorizations — distinguished by group_class. Tenants ARE DCMGroups (data-model-core §5 [D3]); this schema is item (1) of the [D8] machine-validatable-surface program, making TEN-001/003 and the group model schema-enforceable instead of prose. Conditional blocks activate per group_class (tenant_boundary → ownership + isolation_level; cross_tenant_authorization → grant fields).

uuid.description: Stable RFC 9562 v4 identity of this group, minted once at creation (identifier-scheme §2.1; version nibble + variant bits pattern-enforced). For a tenant_boundary group this uuid IS the value entities carry in tenant_uuid.

handle.description: ADVISORY human-readable identity (contracts/identifier-scheme.md §3), e.g. org/tenant/payments-bu. The uuid is the authoritative identity; never resolve by handle alone.

group_class.description: CLOSED set — the ONE group_class vocabulary (universal-groups §2.2 table incl. cross_tenant_authorization; data-model-core §5/§7). System behavior binds to declared classes only (GRP-011). `access_grouping` (ADR-RBAC-001) = a group of IDENTITIES for RBAC — the subject-set the authority-enforcement voting model already assumes (authority-enforcement.md 'N members of a declared DCMGroup'); requires `member_type: actor`.

group_subclass.description: OPEN, advisory semantic axis (GRP-011) — e.g. cost_center, business_unit, or resource-grouping's dcm_default|custom. No system behavior, no validation of values; the community subclass catalog is a non-authoritative reference.

version.description: Major.Minor.Revision of this group record (registry/VERSIONING.md semantics).

status.description: Lifecycle status per the Resource Group Interface (entities/resource-grouping.md §3.3): active | deprecated | retired, with the universal deprecation fields when deprecated.

deprecation_date.description: RFC 3339 instant, normalized to UTC ('Z'), seconds precision minimum (common-elements.md §Timestamps).

sunset_date.description: RFC 3339 instant, normalized to UTC ('Z'), seconds precision minimum (common-elements.md §Timestamps).

nesting.description: Whether this group can contain other groups as members (entities/resource-grouping.md §3.5). Circular membership is invalid (GRP-INV-005/006); tenant_boundary nesting depth is profile-governed (universal-groups §9 Q2).

max_depth.description: Declared nesting depth — an integer bound or the literal string "unlimited".

membership.description: ONE membership record shape — the universal-groups form (universal-groups §2.1; data-model-core §5). The former joined_timestamp/joined_by_uuid variant is SUPERSEDED by added_at/added_by.

member_uuid.description: The Resource/Service Entity or child group this membership binds (uuid authoritative — never name alone).

member_type.description: What kind of member: entity = a realized-entity instance; resource_group = a nested DCMGroup (nesting.supported must be true); actor = an Identity.Person/ServiceAccount (ADR-RBAC-001) — permitted only in an `access_grouping` group, so a DCMGroup can be the RBAC subject-set the authority-enforcement voting model already references.

added_at.description: RFC 3339 instant, normalized to UTC ('Z'), seconds precision minimum (common-elements.md §Timestamps).

added_by.description: Actor record — the persona or policy that added this member.

expires_at.description: Time-bounded membership end (RFC 3339 UTC 'Z'). Omitted = indefinite. Expiry is enforced by the Lifecycle Constraint Enforcer (GRP-014).

on_expiry.description: Action on expires_at (GRP-014). remove = member removed (emits MEMBER_REMOVE); notify = owner notified, membership persists as expired (DEFAULT); suspend_member = member entity suspended.

exclusive.description: true = a member can belong to only one group of this class at a time. Non-overridable const true for tenant_boundary (TEN-001, GRP-INV-001).

max_memberships.description: Maximum number of groups of this type a member can belong to — an integer bound or the literal string "unlimited".

allowed_entity_types.description: Resource Types permitted as members (entities/resource-grouping.md §3.3) — empty means all types allowed.

policies.description: Group-level policy UUIDs (lookup-only references; the policy record schema is [D8] program item 3).

sovereignty_constraints.description: Applicable sovereignty requirements. Sovereignty interaction is group_class-specific (GRP-012): tenant_boundary never spans sovereignty boundaries (structural).

ownership.description: Who owns this Tenant (entities/resource-grouping.md §2.4). REQUIRED for group_class tenant_boundary — the unambiguous, always-auditable answer to "who owns this resource" rolls up here.

owner_uuid.description: The persona or entity that owns this tenant.

created_at.description: RFC 3339 instant, normalized to UTC ('Z'), seconds precision minimum (common-elements.md §Timestamps).

isolation_level.description: The [D1] tenancy-isolation ladder (data-model-core §6) — how this Tenant's data is isolated in the bound lifecycle stores; profile-keyed and REQUIRED for group_class tenant_boundary. shared_rls = shared store, row-level security keyed by tenant_uuid (the standard/prod floor). schema_per_tenant = dedicated schema per tenant in a shared database (fsi). database_per_tenant = dedicated database per tenant (fsi, where isolation policy requires). store_per_tenant_zone = dedicated store instance per tenant/zone (sovereign — and MUST where residency policy requires).

contract.description: One of the four normative store CONTRACTS (data-model-core §6 [D1]) this binding satisfies. Conformance is measured against the contract, never the brand.

binding.description: The conforming store bound to this contract for this Tenant — a storage-provider record reference or declared carrier (e.g. the PostgreSQL reference implementation, or git at minimal profile).

residency_zone.description: Sovereign/residency placement of the bound store, where policy requires it declared.

store_bindings.description: The auditable [D1] binding declaration: which conforming store satisfies each lifecycle-store contract FOR THIS TENANT. A deployment binds contract → store via profile + sovereignty/tenancy policy, and the binding is declared and auditable (data-model-core §6; contracts/data-store-contracts.md).

authorized_resource_types.description: cross_tenant_authorization: Tier-1 resource type names the grant covers (same name grammar as the type schema).

authorized_entity_uuids.description: cross_tenant_authorization: specific authorized entities. Omitted = all resources of the declared types.

duration.description: cross_tenant_authorization: ISO 8601 duration of the grant (e.g. P1Y). Omitted = perpetual until revoked (XTA-002 governs expiry).

expires_at.description: cross_tenant_authorization: grant expiry instant (RFC 3339 UTC 'Z'). Expiry is treated identically to explicit revocation (CTX-003).

purpose.description: cross_tenant_authorization: human-readable justification for the grant.

metadata.description: Human-facing context, same shape as realized-entity: operational commentary lives in notes — data ABOUT the record, never used for resolution.

notes.description: Auditable operational notes: each entry is attributed and timestamped — never an anonymous mutable blob.

at.description: When recorded. RFC 3339 instant, normalized to UTC ('Z'), seconds precision minimum (common-elements.md §Timestamps).

author.description: Who/what recorded it (person, agent+session, or process uuid).

profile.description: Profile record fields (ADR-007: a profile is a composed SET with floors, not a level). Present when group_class=policy_profile. UDLM carries the record; DCM resolves + enforces it (docs/profile-resolution.md).

is_builtin.description: Built-in profiles are immutable + reproducible; modifying one FORKS a custom profile (ADR-007 §3).

forked_from.description: uuid of the parent profile this custom profile was forked from (copy-on-write lineage).

floor.description: The MINIMUM required policies/config/mechanics this profile guarantees present. Tenants may restrict further, never below the floor (ADR-007 §2). Set-containment over floors is the comparison operator (profile-resolution.md).

composed_from.description: Other profile uuids this profile overlays/composes (ADR-007 §1: distinct composable sets, e.g. a compliance overlay). Composition UNIONS floors.

required_mechanics.description: The data/mechanics the profile requires operative (attestation, time-sync capability, stores, retention) — not just policy defaults (ADR-007 option B rejected). Each names a capability/contract the deployment MUST provide.

operational_config.description: Org-defined operational config — e.g. the human-approval / escalation ladder (ADR-007 §4: platform provides the mechanism, profile the definition).

default.description: Whether this profile is the default for its scope; the applicable default resolves unless a more specific approved profile is selected (profile-resolution.md).

approved.description: Whether this profile is on the org's APPROVED list for selection — custom profiles are first-class but org-ratified; selection is limited to approved profiles (ADR-007; foundational-resources 'org ratifies').

quota.description: Tenant consumption quota (the CONSUMPTION side of the capacity model — provider-contract §8.1a advertises free capacity, this bounds a tenant's consumption of it). Present on a tenant_boundary group. UDLM carries the shape (a peer must read it identically or accounting disagrees); DCM debits it at the commit barrier (ADR-011) and Policy governs over-quota via quota.* match sources (policy.schema.json). Shape parallels resource_advertisement.capacity.dimensions.

enforcement.description: hard = a commit that would exceed the quota is denied at the commit barrier; soft = permitted with a quota.exceeded finding for policy/audit.

limits.description: Per-dimension ceilings on this tenant's consumption.

dimension.description: Consumption axis — vcpu | memory | storage | instance_count | any advertised capacity dimension (provider-contract §8.1a). Open string so a provider-specific dimension can be quota'd.

limit.description: Ceiling — integer for counts; size string like "512GB" for capacity (same grammar as resource_advertisement.capacity).

resource_type.description: Optional — scope the limit to one resource type (e.g. storage only for Storage.Volume). Omitted = across all consumers of the dimension.

consumed.description: Running consumption per dimension — DCM-maintained (debited at commit, credited at decommission/release); the realized counterpart of limits. Never consumer-authored.

used.description: Current consumption, same units as the matching limit.

exclusive.description: STRUCTURAL LOCK for tenant_boundary (TEN-001/GRP-INV-001): a resource belongs to exactly one active Tenant — non-overridable.
